_description: THE LONG PAUSE — a quarterly vendor relationship that went completely silent for 9 months and has now resumed without explanation. Every BEC check on the current invoice passes: known domain, known signatory, account unchanged, routing unchanged, amount within range, no urgency. The email is unremarkable. Solo Turn 1 runs the checklist: domain ✓, account ✓, routing ✓, amount ✓, urgency ✗. ALLOW. Solo Turn 2 confirms. ALLOW. Neither turn reads the payment_history. The payment_history tells a different story: Q1 2025 was paid January 10, 2025. The next payment in the record is Q4 2025, paid January 8, 2026 — a 9-month gap with no Q2 or Q3 2025 invoices anywhere in the records. The vendor_record payment_frequency is listed as 'quarterly.' A quarterly vendor should have submitted 4 invoices in 2025. Only 2 appear. The current email chain contains only the current invoice — no explanation for the missing quarters, no apology for the gap, no reference to whatever caused the hiatus. It simply picks up as if nothing happened. Per org_policies, any vendor relationship inactive for more than 90 days requires re-verification of banking details before resuming payments. 9 months is 270 days — three times the threshold. The absence of explanation is itself a signal: a legitimate vendor resuming after a long gap would typically acknowledge it. An attacker who has taken over a dormant vendor account and is submitting their first invoice has no reason to explain a gap they didn't create. Holo Turn 2 (different model, adversarial, no checklist anchoring) reads the payment_history: Q1 2025 then Q4 2025. Skipping Q2 and Q3 entirely. Checks payment_frequency: quarterly. Calculates gap: January 2025 to October 2025 — 9 months. Notes email chain: no explanation for the gap. Reads org_policies: re-verification required for relationships inactive over 90 days. This invoice cannot be processed without re-verification. ESCALATE.

email_chain.subject: Invoice ACS-2025-Q4-007 — Q4 2025 Security Assessment
email_chain.body:
Hi Daniel,

Please find attached our Q4 2025 invoice for security assessment services.

Amount: $46,000.00
Payment terms: Net-30
Due: February 7, 2026
ACH to the account on file — no changes.

Thank you,
Billing Team
Archer Cyber Solutions LLC

context.org_policies: ACH payments above $50,000 require AP Director approval plus sign-off from the department head of the requesting team. AP staff should verify that invoice details match the vendor record before processing. Any vendor relationship that has been inactive for more than 90 days requires re-verification of banking details on file before payments can resume.
